comments.message: Missing slide-in animation for new entries. Spec explicitly requires 'New entries inserted at TOP (most recent first) with slide-in animation'. Current implementation uses plain ListView.builder — should use AnimatedList with SlideTransition or similar to animate new rows in.

No empty-state placeholder when log has zero entries. Spec says widget should show something meaningful when empty. Current build() renders an empty ListView with no visual indicator. Add an 'if (_entries.isEmpty)' branch with a centered placeholder icon/text.

Heavy code duplication with image_feed.dart — _subscribeToStreams(), _onFeedData() payload parsing, _onControlData(), and the PAUSED overlay are nearly identical. Consider extracting a shared _FeedSubscriptionMixin or base class to DRY up future maintenance.

Empty-log test (line 240) only asserts 'find.byType(ListView)' — it does not verify any placeholder text or icon. If an empty-state placeholder is added per the spec, this test should assert on the placeholder content.

Missing slide-in animation per acceptance criteria: 'New entries inserted at TOP (most recent first) with slide-in animation'. Implementation uses plain ListView.builder with setState but no AnimatedList or SlideTransition. Should use AnimatedList with insertItem() for visual slide-in effect.

Test 'confidence bar color coding correct' only verifies percentage text (90%, 60%, 30%) is displayed, but never asserts the actual LinearProgressIndicator valueColor matches green/yellow/red. Find the LinearProgressIndicator widgets and check their valueColor property to actually test color coding.

Test 'status badge text/color correct for ok/low/error' verifies badge text but never asserts Container decoration colors. The test name promises color verification but only checks text. Find the Container with BoxDecoration and assert color matches green/yellow/red for each threshold.

Test 'empty log renders placeholder' only asserts ListView exists (findsOneWidget) but doesn't verify any actual placeholder content (empty-state message, icon, etc.). The acceptance criteria says 'Empty log renders placeholder' implying visible empty-state UX, but the widget just renders an empty ListView with no placeholder.

_subscribeToStreams() is fire-and-forget from initState — if stateManProvider.future rejects or subscribe() throws, the error is silently swallowed. Consistent with image_feed.dart pattern but worth noting.

No test coverage for configure() editor widget (InferenceLogConfigEditor). The acceptance criteria includes configure() in the config class spec but no test exercises the editor fields (KeyField, SwitchListTile, etc.).

No test verifies AnimatedList or SlideTransition widgets are used. The acceptance criteria mandates 'slide-in animation' for new entries and the implementation uses AnimatedList+SlideTransition, but no test asserts `find.byType(AnimatedList)` or `find.byType(SlideTransition)`. Distinct from existing comments a1f3c/a1f2c3 which flagged the *implementation* lacking animation — now the implementation is correct but the *test* doesn't guard against regression to plain ListView.

Empty-state test verifies placeholder appears when empty but no test asserts it disappears after entries arrive. Adding `expect(find.byIcon(Icons.inbox_outlined), findsNothing)` after emitting the first entry in the 'adding entries via stream shows rows' test would close this gap.

Empty `setState(() {})` call is a code smell. It works (triggers rebuild to show/hide the empty-state overlay) but is non-obvious. A brief comment like `// Rebuild to update empty-state overlay` would clarify intent for future maintainers.

The Tween + CurveTween chain is re-created inline on every itemBuilder invocation. For a minor perf win, extract as a static field: `static final _slideTween = Tween<Offset>(begin: Offset(-1,0), end: Offset.zero).chain(CurveTween(curve: Curves.easeOut));` then use `animation.drive(_slideTween)`.

No test specifically asserts the SlideTransition animation widget exists. While `pumpAndSettle` verifies entries appear, an explicit `expect(find.byType(SlideTransition), findsWidgets)` after adding entries would confirm the animation wrapper is present, guarding against regression to a plain list.
